steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses with the same subject (The resulting course): was 2,500 miles long and modeled a vision of the EU’s unifying aims. The blank must join these two clauses while fitting in the detail about crossing borders. The correct answer will use Standard English punctuation and structure.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'and, crossed': “and, crossed seven separate borders” places a comma after and before the verb, which is not a valid participial phrase construction. The past tense crossed also creates a third predicate that is not properly coordinated. ✗
  • Choice B — 'and had crossed': “and had crossed” adds a second finite verb in the past perfect, implying the crossing happened before being 2,500 miles long — that sequence makes no sense here, and it leaves modeled without a connector. ✗
  • Choice C — 'and crossed': “and crossed seven separate borders of neighboring countries, modeled” strings three past-tense verbs — was, crossed, and modeled — separated only by a comma before the last one. That comma-splice structure is not Standard English. ✗
  • Choice D — 'and, crossing': “and, crossing seven separate borders of neighboring countries, modeled” inserts a participial phrase between and and the second main verb modeled. The commas correctly set off the participial phrase, and the two independent clauses are properly joined with and. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
